# services.tcsd

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.tcsd`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.tcsd.conformanceCred` | `absolute path` | Path to the conformance credential for your TPM. See also the platformCred option |
| `services.tcsd.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable tcsd, a Trusted Computing management service that provides TCG Software Stack (TSS). The tcsd daemon is the only portal to the Trusted Platform Module (TPM), a hardware chip on the motherboard. |
| `services.tcsd.endorsementCred` | `absolute path` | Path to the endorsement credential for your TPM. See also the platformCred option |
| `services.tcsd.firmwarePCRs` | `string` | PCR indices used in the TPM for firmware measurements. |
| `services.tcsd.group` | `string` | Group account under which tcsd runs. |
| `services.tcsd.kernelPCRs` | `string` | PCR indices used in the TPM for kernel measurements. |
| `services.tcsd.platformCred` | `absolute path` | Path to the platform credential for your TPM. Your TPM manufacturer may have provided you with a set of credentials (certificates) that should be used when creating identities using your TPM. When a user of your TPM makes an identity, this credential will be encrypted as part of that process. See the 1.1b TPM Main specification section 9.3 for information on this process. |
| `services.tcsd.stateDir` | `absolute path` | The location of the system persistent storage file. The system persistent storage file holds keys and data across restarts of the TCSD and system reboots. |
| `services.tcsd.user` | `string` | User account under which tcsd runs. |
